warnings.long_duration: Estimated duration exceeds {{max_days}} days. Consider increasing traffic or relaxing MDE.
warnings.low_traffic: Effective test traffic is low. Duration and variance risk may be high.
warnings.missing_variance: Continuous metric requires std_dev for deterministic sample size calculation.
warnings.many_variants_low_traffic: Too many variants for the available traffic. Consider reducing the number of variants.
warnings.seasonality_present: Seasonality is present. Cover at least one full weekly cycle.
warnings.campaign_contamination: Active campaigns may contaminate the test and bias uplift estimates.
warnings.underpowered_design: Requested power is below the usual 0.8 threshold. Risk of false negatives is higher.
warnings.conservative_multivariant_alpha: More than two variants trigger a Bonferroni alpha correction. This is conservative and may overstate the required sample size.
warnings.long_test_not_possible: Expected duration is long, but the project cannot support a long-running test.
warnings.interim_looks_increase_sample: Sequential design with {{n_looks}} looks increases required sample size by ~{{pct}}%
warnings.srm_detected: Sample ratio mismatch detected (p < 0.001). Check randomization and tracking.
warnings.cluster_randomization: Randomization unit is a cluster (e.g. store, region, classroom), not an individual user. Naive standard errors assume independent observations and are underestimated under cluster randomization — treat significance as optimistic until a design-effect/ICC adjustment or cluster-robust analysis is applied.

report.executive_summary: {{project_name}} tests whether {{change_description}} can improve {{primary_metric_name}} for {{target_audience}}. The deterministic plan estimates {{estimated_duration_days}} days with {{sample_size_per_variant}} users per variant.

guardrails.binary_note: With N={{sample_size_per_variant}} per variant, can detect >= {{detectable_mde}} pp change at 80% power
guardrails.continuous_note: With N={{sample_size_per_variant}} per variant, can detect >= {{detectable_mde}} change at 80% power

risks.no_major_warnings: No major deterministic warnings identified at this stage.
risks.product: Expected result depends on the hypothesis that {{desired_result}}.

recommendations.before_launch_default: Validate tracking and assignment before exposing live traffic.
recommendations.during_test_monitor: Monitor guardrail metrics and sample accumulation daily.
recommendations.during_test_avoid_early_stop: Avoid stopping the test early on short-term volatility.
recommendations.after_test_interpret: Interpret lift together with guardrail and diagnostic metrics.
recommendations.after_test_segment: Document segmentation effects before rollout decisions.

open_questions.validate: How will the team validate {{what_to_validate}} before launch?
open_questions.market_stability: Is the market context '{{market}}' stable during the planned test window?

schemas.continuous_guardrail_requires_mean_and_s: continuous guardrail requires baseline_mean and std_dev
schemas.binary_baseline_range: baseline_value must be between 0 and 1 for binary metrics
schemas.continuous_baseline_positive: baseline_value must be positive for continuous metrics
schemas.continuous_std_positive: std_dev must be positive for continuous metrics
schemas.ratio_baseline_positive: baseline_value (baseline ratio) must be positive for ratio metrics
schemas.count_baseline_positive: baseline_value (baseline event rate) must be positive for count metrics
schemas.cluster_size_min: avg_cluster_size (average cluster size) must be at least 1
schemas.icc_range: icc (intraclass correlation coefficient) must be between 0 and 1
schemas.ratio_std_positive: std_dev (per-user linearized standard deviation) must be positive for ratio metrics
schemas.binary_rejects_continuous_data: binary metric_type does not accept continuous data
schemas.continuous_rejects_binary_data: continuous metric_type does not accept binary data
schemas.ranked_values_finite: ranked sample values must be finite numbers
schemas.mann_whitney_requires_ranked_data: mann_whitney metric_type requires ranked (raw sample) data
schemas.mann_whitney_rejects_other_data: mann_whitney metric_type does not accept binary or continuous data
schemas.actual_counts_positive_sum: actual_counts must sum to a positive total
schemas.baseline_rate_range: baseline_rate must be between 0 and 100 for binary metrics
schemas.baseline_mean_positive: baseline_mean must be positive for continuous metrics
schemas.ratio_baseline_mean_positive: baseline_mean (baseline ratio) must be positive for ratio metrics
schemas.power_values_range: power_values must contain values between 0 and 1
schemas.observed_expected_same_length: observed_counts and expected_fractions must have same length
schemas.observed_counts_positive_sum: observed_counts must sum to a positive total
schemas.bandit_arm_rates_range: Arm conversion rates must each be between 0 and 1.
schemas.namespace_range_order: namespace range_start must be less than range_end
schemas.targeting_in_requires_list: targeting operator 'in' requires a list value
schemas.targeting_numeric_requires_number: numeric targeting operators require a numeric value
schemas.fisher_exact_requires_binary_data: fisher_exact metric_type requires binary (2x2 count) data
schemas.fisher_exact_rejects_other_data: fisher_exact metric_type does not accept continuous or ranked data
schemas.fisher_exact_table_too_large: fisher_exact is intended for small samples; this table is too large — use the binary (normal-approximation) test
schemas.count_requires_count_data: count metric_type requires count (events over exposure) data
schemas.count_rejects_other_data: count metric_type does not accept binary, continuous or ranked data
schemas.count_events_too_large: count: too many total events for the exact test — the normal approximation is exact at this scale
schemas.bootstrap_requires_ranked_data: bootstrap metric_type requires ranked (raw sample) data
schemas.bootstrap_rejects_other_data: bootstrap metric_type does not accept binary or continuous data
schemas.quantile_requires_ranked_data: quantile metric_type requires ranked (raw sample) data
schemas.quantile_rejects_other_data: quantile metric_type does not accept binary or continuous data
schemas.trimmed_t_requires_ranked_data: trimmed_t metric_type requires ranked (raw sample) data
schemas.trimmed_t_rejects_other_data: trimmed_t metric_type does not accept binary or continuous data
schemas.equivalence_requires_continuous_data: equivalence metric_type requires continuous (summary statistics) data
schemas.equivalence_rejects_other_data: equivalence metric_type does not accept binary or ranked data
schemas.equivalence_requires_margin: equivalence metric_type requires an equivalence_margin greater than zero
schemas.categorical_min_columns: contingency table needs at least two columns
schemas.categorical_max_columns: contingency table has too many columns
schemas.categorical_rectangular: contingency table must be rectangular (every row needs the same number of columns)
schemas.categorical_non_negative: contingency table counts must be non-negative
schemas.planned_test_metric_mismatch: planned_test is not available for this metric_type
schemas.tost_margin_required: equivalence_margin_pct is required for a TOST equivalence plan
schemas.paired_length_mismatch: paired samples must have the same number of control and treatment values
schemas.paired_values_finite: paired sample values must be finite numbers
schemas.paired_mcnemar_binary: McNemar's test requires paired binary values (each value must be 0 or 1)
schemas.paired_t_degenerate: the paired t-test is undefined: it needs at least two pairs with a non-zero difference variance
schemas.wilcoxon_degenerate: the Wilcoxon signed-rank test is undefined: it needs at least two non-zero differences that are not all tied in magnitude
schemas.omnibus_min_group_size: each group needs at least two observations
schemas.omnibus_group_too_large: a group has too many observations
schemas.omnibus_values_finite: omnibus values must be finite numbers
schemas.welch_anova_degenerate: Welch's ANOVA is undefined: every group needs at least two observations and non-zero within-group variance
schemas.kruskal_wallis_degenerate: the Kruskal–Wallis test is undefined: the observations carry no rank variation (every value is identical)

errors.srm_detected: SRM detected - check your randomization or tracking implementation

verdict.no_difference: No statistically significant difference at alpha={{alpha}}
verdict.not_equivalent: Equivalence not demonstrated within ±{{margin}} at alpha={{alpha}}

interpretation.binary: Treatment conversion {{treatment}}% vs control {{control}}%. Absolute effect {{effect}} pp with {{ciLevel}}% CI [{{ciLower}}, {{ciUpper}}] pp. Two-sided p-value {{pValue}}; result is {{significance}}.
interpretation.continuous: Treatment mean {{treatmentMean}} vs control {{controlMean}}. Effect {{effect}} with {{ciLevel}}% CI [{{ciLower}}, {{ciUpper}}]. Two-sided p-value {{pValue}}.
interpretation.ratio: Treatment ratio {{treatmentRatio}} vs control {{controlRatio}}. Absolute effect {{effect}} with {{ciLevel}}% CI [{{ciLower}}, {{ciUpper}}]. Two-sided p-value {{pValue}} (delta method); result is {{significance}}.
interpretation.mann_whitney: Treatment median {{treatmentMedian}} vs control {{controlMedian}}. Hodges–Lehmann shift {{shift}} with {{ciLevel}}% CI [{{ciLower}}, {{ciUpper}}]. P(treatment > control) = {{cles}}%. Mann–Whitney U {{uStatistic}}, two-sided p-value {{pValue}} ({{method}}); result is {{significance}}.
interpretation.degenerate: Observed inputs are degenerate, so the test statistic cannot be computed.
interpretation.fisher_exact: Treatment conversion {{treatment}}% vs control {{control}}%. Absolute effect {{effect}} pp. Odds ratio {{oddsRatio}}. Fisher's exact two-sided p-value {{pValue}}; result is {{significance}}.
interpretation.count: Treatment rate {{treatmentRate}} vs control {{controlRate}}. Rate ratio {{rateRatio}}. Rate difference {{rateDifference}} with {{ciLevel}}% CI [{{ciLower}}, {{ciUpper}}]. Poisson exact two-sided p-value {{pValue}}; result is {{significance}}.
interpretation.bootstrap: Treatment mean {{treatmentMean}} vs control {{controlMean}}. Mean difference {{effect}} with {{ciLevel}}% percentile bootstrap CI [{{ciLower}}, {{ciUpper}}]. Permutation two-sided p-value {{pValue}} over {{permutations}} relabellings; result is {{significance}}.
interpretation.quantile: Treatment P{{percentile}} quantile {{treatmentQuantile}} vs control {{controlQuantile}}. Quantile shift {{effect}} with {{ciLevel}}% percentile bootstrap CI [{{ciLower}}, {{ciUpper}}]. Permutation two-sided p-value {{pValue}} over {{permutations}} relabellings; result is {{significance}}.
interpretation.trimmed_t: Treatment {{trimPct}}%-trimmed mean {{treatmentMean}} vs control {{controlMean}} (effective n {{treatmentEffectiveN}} vs {{controlEffectiveN}} after trimming). Robust mean shift {{effect}} with {{ciLevel}}% CI [{{ciLower}}, {{ciUpper}}]. Yuen–Welch two-sided t p-value {{pValue}} on {{df}} df; result is {{significance}}.
interpretation.equivalence: Treatment mean {{treatmentMean}} vs control {{controlMean}}. Effect {{effect}} tested against an equivalence margin of ±{{margin}}: {{equivalence}}. The {{ciLevel}}% confidence interval [{{ciLower}}, {{ciUpper}}] is the TOST decision interval; two one-sided p-value {{pValue}}.
interpretation.categorical: Chi-square {{chiSquare}} on {{df}} degrees of freedom for a {{rows}}×{{cols}} contingency table (N = {{n}}). Two-sided p-value {{pValue}}; result is {{significance}}. Cramér's V {{cramersV}} measures the strength of the association.
interpretation.paired_t: Treatment mean {{treatmentMean}} vs control {{controlMean}}. Mean paired difference {{meanDiff}} with {{ciLevel}}% CI [{{ciLower}}, {{ciUpper}}]. Paired t {{tStat}} on {{df}} degrees of freedom, two-sided p-value {{pValue}}; result is {{significance}}. Cohen's dz {{cohenDz}}.
interpretation.wilcoxon: Hodges–Lehmann pseudomedian of the paired differences {{pseudomedian}} with {{ciLevel}}% CI [{{ciLower}}, {{ciUpper}}]. Signed-rank statistic {{wStat}} over {{nNonzero}} non-zero differences ({{nZero}} zero differences dropped), two-sided p-value {{pValue}}; result is {{significance}}. Rank-biserial correlation {{rankBiserial}}.
interpretation.mcnemar: Discordant pairs: {{b}} switched to success, {{c}} switched away ({{nDiscordant}} total). Marginal proportion difference {{proportionDiff}} pp, discordance odds ratio {{oddsRatio}}. Two-sided p-value {{pValue}} ({{method}}); result is {{significance}}.
interpretation.welch_anova: Welch's ANOVA F {{fStatistic}} on {{dfNum}} and {{dfDen}} degrees of freedom across {{groups}} groups (N = {{n}}). Two-sided p-value {{pValue}}; result is {{significance}}. η² {{etaSquared}} of the variance is explained by group membership.
interpretation.kruskal_wallis: Kruskal–Wallis H {{hStatistic}} on {{df}} degrees of freedom across {{groups}} groups (N = {{n}}). Chi-square p-value {{pValue}}; result is {{significance}}. ε² {{epsilonSquared}} measures the rank variation explained by group membership.

omnibus.verdict_difference: At least one group differs at alpha={{alpha}}
omnibus.verdict_no_difference: No significant difference between groups at alpha={{alpha}}

live_stats.disclaimer: MVP execution layer — a full plan -> run -> analyze cycle demonstration, not built for high-traffic production. Live stats are recomputed on demand over deduplicated exposures and conversions.

srm.insufficient_data: Not enough exposures yet to check sample-ratio mismatch.
srm.detected: Sample-ratio mismatch detected (p < 0.001) — assignment looks broken; do not trust results.
srm.ok: No sample-ratio mismatch; traffic split matches the design.

comparison.insufficient_data: Each arm needs at least 2 exposed users before a test can run.
comparison.continuous_zero_variance: Per-user values show zero variance in an arm, so a t-test cannot be computed yet.
comparison.ratio_insufficient_data: Each arm needs at least 2 exposed users before a ratio test can run.
comparison.ratio_degenerate: The ratio variance is degenerate in an arm (zero denominator or no variation across users), so a delta-method test cannot be computed yet.
comparison.bonferroni_note: Bonferroni-adjusted alpha {{adjusted_alpha}} across {{comparison_count}} treatment-vs-control comparisons (nominal alpha {{nominal_alpha}}); significance and the confidence interval use this adjusted level to control the family-wise error rate.

always_valid.not_evaluable: Not enough variation in the arms yet to form an anytime-valid interval.
always_valid.ok: Anytime-valid mSPRT view: this p-value and confidence sequence stay valid under continuous monitoring, so you may stop as soon as the sequence excludes zero without inflating the false-positive rate.

sequential.unsupported_metric: Sequential planning is not available for this metric type yet, so the O'Brien-Fleming boundary cannot be evaluated.
sequential.fixed_horizon: Fixed-horizon design (n_looks=1): read the frequentist p-value only once at the planned sample size — peeking early inflates the false-positive rate.
sequential.insufficient_data: Not enough exposures yet to evaluate the sequential boundary.
sequential.active: O'Brien-Fleming sequential boundary at the current information fraction. A treatment is sequential-significant only when |z| exceeds this (stricter early, ~nominal at the end). The boundary strictly controls alpha only at the pre-planned looks — reading this repeatedly at arbitrary fractions is continuous peeking, so treat any between-look crossing as provisional until a planned look.

cuped.not_applicable: Live CUPED applies to continuous metrics — the adjusted outcome is analysed with the continuous t-test. This experiment uses a binary metric, so CUPED is not applied here.
cuped.unavailable: CUPED needs per-user pre-experiment covariates. Ingest pre-period values via POST /api/v1/experiments/{id}/pre-period to enable variance reduction on live data.
cuped.too_many: More distinct pre-period covariates were ingested than live CUPED supports. Reduce the number of covariate names to enable the multi-covariate adjustment.
cuped.available: CUPED-adjusted estimates: Y_adj = Y - theta^T(X - mean X), the regression coefficients theta = Sigma_xx^-1 Sigma_xy pooled across arms. The adjusted outcome is analysed with the continuous t-test; the effect is unchanged in expectation while variance drops with the covariates' correlation. Estimated only over users that carry the full covariate vector, so it describes that subpopulation.
cuped.insufficient_data: Each arm needs at least 2 users with a pre-period covariate.
cuped.zero_variance: CUPED-adjusted values show zero variance in an arm, so a t-test cannot be computed yet.

stratified.not_applicable: Post-stratification applies to binary and continuous metrics; this experiment's metric type is not supported here.
stratified.unavailable: Post-stratification needs a per-user stratum. Ingest strata via POST /api/v1/experiments/{id}/strata to reduce variance when the stratum explains the outcome.
stratified.too_many: More distinct strata were ingested than post-stratification supports. Too many sparse strata increase variance; reduce the number of strata to enable the adjustment.
stratified.available: Post-stratified estimate: the effect is computed within each stratum and recombined weighted by stratum size (Delta = sum (n_s/N)*Delta_s). Variance drops when the stratum explains outcome variation, and can rise with many sparse strata, so the reduction is reported honestly. Estimated over exposed users that carry a stratum.
stratified.insufficient_data: No stratum has both arms populated with at least 2 users yet, so a post-stratified test cannot be computed.

guardrail.none: No guardrail metrics are declared for this experiment. Add guardrail metrics in the design to monitor protected metrics (latency, error rate, revenue) for regressions on live data.
guardrail.unavailable: Guardrail metrics are declared but no guardrail outcomes have been ingested yet. Send guardrail events through POST /api/v1/experiments/{id}/conversions using each guardrail's name as the conversion metric.
guardrail.ok: No guardrail metric shows a significant regression beyond its tolerance margin.
guardrail.warning: A guardrail metric is degrading past its tolerance margin but not yet significantly — watch it before shipping.
guardrail.breached: A guardrail metric is significantly degraded beyond its tolerance margin. Shipping is blocked until the regression is understood.
guardrail.insufficient_data: Each arm needs at least 2 exposed users with guardrail data before a breach test can run.
guardrail.zero_variance: Guardrail values show zero variance in an arm, so a breach test cannot be computed yet.

holdout.unavailable_ratio: Holdout analysis applies to binary and continuous metrics; this experiment uses a ratio metric.
holdout.unavailable: No holdout users have been ingested yet. Hold users back from the rollout via POST /api/v1/experiments/{id}/holdout to measure the rollout's cumulative effect against a held-back group; their outcomes ride the ordinary conversion stream under the primary metric.
holdout.insufficient_data: Each side (the pooled treated arms and the holdout) needs at least 2 users with usable variation before the cumulative test can run.
holdout.ok: Cumulative effect of the rollout: the pooled treated arms (variation_index >= 1) vs the held-back holdout group on the primary metric. This is the standing effect of everything shipped — a check on the per-variant wins — reusing the same frequentist, Bayesian and anytime-valid views.
holdout.zero_variance: Per-user values show zero variance in the treated or holdout group, so a t-test cannot be computed yet.
